ups/versions

Source   Edit  

Imports

spec

Types

Operator = enum
  Tag = "#", Wild = "*", Tilde = "~", Caret = "^", Equal = "==", AtLeast = ">=",
  Over = ">", Under = "<", NotMore = "<="
operators affect the bearing of a release Source   Edit   
Release = object
  case kind*: Operator
  of Tag:
    reference*: string
  of Wild, Caret, Tilde:
    accepts*: VersionMask
  of Equal, AtLeast, Over, Under, NotMore:
    version*: Version
the specification of a version, release, or mask Source   Edit   
Version = object
  major*: VersionField
  minor*: VersionField
  patch*: VersionField
Source   Edit   
VersionField = uint
Source   Edit   
VersionIndex = range[0 .. 2]
Source   Edit   
VersionMask = object
  major*: VersionMaskField
  minor*: VersionMaskField
  patch*: VersionMaskField
a version mask may apply to any or all fields Source   Edit   
VersionMaskField = Option[VersionField]
Source   Edit  

Consts

nimVersion = (major: 2'u, minor: 1'u, patch: 1'u)
Source   Edit   
Wildlings = {Wild, Caret, Tilde}
mavericks of the version world Source   Edit  

Procs

proc `$`(field: VersionMaskField): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `$`(mask: VersionMask): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `$`(spec: Release): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `$`(v: Version): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `<`(a, b: Release): bool {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `<`(a, b: Version): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `<`(a, b: VersionMaskField): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `<=`(a, b: Release): bool {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `<=`(a, b: Version): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a, b: Release): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a, b: Version): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a, b: VersionMask): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a, b: VersionMaskField): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a: VersionMask; b: Version): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `[]=`(mask: var VersionMask; index: VersionIndex; value: VersionMaskField) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc acceptable(mask: VersionMaskField; op: Operator; value: VersionField): bool {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
true if the versionfield value passes the mask Source   Edit   
proc at[T: Version | VersionMask](version: T; index: VersionIndex): auto
an indexing operator which cannot clash Source   Edit   
proc bumpVersion(ver: Version; major = false; minor = false; patch = false): Option[
    Version] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
increment the version by the specified metric; returns none() if no bump occurred Source   Edit   
proc effectively(mask: VersionMask): Version {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
replace * with 0 in wildcard masks Source   Edit   
proc effectively(release: Release): Version {....raises: [Exception, ValueError],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
convert a release to a version for rough comparisons Source   Edit   
proc hash(field: VersionMaskField): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
help hash version masks Source   Edit   
proc hash(mask: VersionMask): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
uniquely identify a version mask Source   Edit   
proc hash(release: Release): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
uniquely identify a release Source   Edit   
proc hash(v: Version): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc isSpecific(release: Release): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
if the version/match specifies a full X.Y.Z version Source   Edit   
proc isValid(release: Release): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
true if the release seems plausible Source   Edit   
proc isValid(v: Version): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
true if the version seems legit Source   Edit   
proc newRelease(reference: string; operator = Equal): Release {.
    ...raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc newRelease(version: Version): Release {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
create a new release using a version Source   Edit   
proc omitStars(mask: VersionMask): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc parseDottedVersion(input: string): Option[Version] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
try to parse 1.2.3 into a Version; we'll ignore any extra values, but the first three must parse Source   Edit   
proc parseVersionLoosely(content: string): Option[Release] {.
    ...raises: [Exception, ValueError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
a very relaxed parser for versions found in tags, etc. only valid releases are emitted, however Source   Edit   
proc specifically(release: Release): Version {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
a full X.Y.Z version the release will match Source   Edit   
proc toMask(version: Version): VersionMask {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
populate a versionmask with values from a version Source   Edit  

Iterators

iterator items[T: Version | VersionMask](version: T): auto
Source   Edit   
iterator likelyTags(version: Version): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
produce tags with/without silly v prefixes Source   Edit   
iterator pairs[T: Version | VersionMask](version: T): auto
Source   Edit   
iterator semanticVersionStrings(mask: VersionMask): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
emit 3, 3.1, 3.1.4 (if possible) Source   Edit   
iterator semanticVersionStrings(version: Version): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
emit 3, 3.1, 3.1.4 Source   Edit  

Macros

macro V(a, b, c: typed): untyped
compose a Version object more simply; this massages the fields. Source   Edit
